hypothesis: Joint contrastive pretraining that aligns 3D electron density grids with multiple complementary orientation-invariant molecular descriptors (e.g., canonical SMILES, InChI, and molecular formula) will produce embeddings that are both SO(3)-invariant and robust across distinct low-energy conformers—yielding higher multi-pose and multi-conformer retrieval rates and lower QM9 linear-probe MAEs—compared to pretraining with a single symbolic anchor.
